raw_response: {"rating": 3.8, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is a news report. The author’s own framing repeatedly praises deregulation and disparages environmental regulations, indicating a rightward lean; embedded link headlines also reinforce a pro-deregulatory, anti-blue-state frame. The consistent directional choices warrant a Right rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The lead asserts the rescission will be \"saving Americans billions\" as a statement of fact, praising deregulation without attribution or balancing context; (2) The finding is labeled \"Obama\" and described as leading to a \"slew of new restrictions and regulations,\" a loaded, negative portrayal of regulation; (3) The author claims the 2012 start-stop credit \"quickly infuriated motorists,\" an emotionally charged generalization presented in the author’s voice; (4) The start-stop credit is characterized as a \"regulatory loophole\" that failed to deliver empirical or health benefits, a contested claim asserted by the author without sourcing; (5) Embedded link headlines selected by the author/editor—e.g., \"TRUMP TAKES AXE TO FEDERAL RED TAPE,\" \"ZELDIN OVERHAULS BIDEN-ERA WATER RULE...,\" and \"TEXAS CHALLENGES NEWSOM’S GAS CAR CRACKDOWN\"—signal pro-deregulation and anti-California/Biden framing; (6) Opposition is minimized in the author’s prose with phrases like \"the climate change field appears eager to litigate\" and \"Some states, however, were not supportive,\" which downplay counterarguments relative to the detailed, affirmative deregulation framing earlier in the piece."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report. The author’s own framing repeatedly praises deregulation and disparages environmental regulations, indicating a rightward lean; embedded link headlines also reinforce a pro-deregulatory, anti-blue-state frame. The consistent directional choices warrant a Right rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The lead asserts the rescission will be "saving Americans billions" as a statement of fact, praising deregulation without attribution or balancing context; (2) The finding is labeled "Obama" and described as leading to a "slew of new restrictions and regulations," a loaded, negative portrayal of regulation; (3) The author claims the 2012 start-stop credit "quickly infuriated motorists," an emotionally charged generalization presented in the author’s voice; (4) The start-stop credit is characterized as a "regulatory loophole" that failed to deliver empirical or health benefits, a contested claim asserted by the author without sourcing; (5) Embedded link headlines selected by the author/editor—e.g., "TRUMP TAKES AXE TO FEDERAL RED TAPE," "ZELDIN OVERHAULS BIDEN-ERA WATER RULE...," and "TEXAS CHALLENGES NEWSOM’S GAS CAR CRACKDOWN"—signal pro-deregulation and anti-California/Biden framing; (6) Opposition is minimized in the author’s prose with phrases like "the climate change field appears eager to litigate" and "Some states, however, were not supportive," which downplay counterarguments relative to the detailed, affirmative deregulation framing earlier in the piece.

explanation: This is a news report. The author’s own framing repeatedly praises deregulation and disparages environmental regulations, indicating a rightward lean; embedded link headlines also reinforce a pro-deregulatory, anti-blue-state frame. The consistent directional choices warrant a Right rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The lead asserts the rescission will be "saving Americans billions" as a statement of fact, praising deregulation without attribution or balancing context; (2) The finding is labeled "Obama" and described as leading to a "slew of new restrictions and regulations," a loaded, negative portrayal of regulation; (3) The author claims the 2012 start-stop credit "quickly infuriated motorists," an emotionally charged generalization presented in the author’s voice; (4) The start-stop credit is characterized as a "regulatory loophole" that failed to deliver empirical or health benefits, a contested claim asserted by the author without sourcing; (5) Embedded link headlines selected by the author/editor—e.g., "TRUMP TAKES AXE TO FEDERAL RED TAPE," "ZELDIN OVERHAULS BIDEN-ERA WATER RULE...," and "TEXAS CHALLENGES NEWSOM’S GAS CAR CRACKDOWN"—signal pro-deregulation and anti-California/Biden framing; (6) Opposition is minimized in the author’s prose with phrases like "the climate change field appears eager to litigate" and "Some states, however, were not supportive," which downplay counterarguments relative to the detailed, affirmative deregulation framing earlier in the piece.
